description:
At `Term::CallClosure(v, args, cont)` inside the queried-set walk, when `fn_constants[v] = F` is known:
- Register `(F, typed_args)` in the queried-set just like a direct call would.
- Skip registering F's any-key from the originating MakeClosure when the closure value's only sinks are resolvable CallClosure sites (i.e., every CallClosure on this var has `fn_constants[v]` populated).

Depends on .29.10.1 (fn_constants must exist).

## Scope

- `src/ir_typer.rs` queried-set walk (around the MakeClosure handling at line 422-437 and cont-site handling): branch on fn_constants for CallClosure/TailCallClosure.
- The MakeClosure-registration site stays, but is gated: when MakeClosure is `(F, [])` AND every CallClosure consumer of that var has fn_constants known to F, omit the `(F, [any...])` query.
- The "every consumer resolvable" check is local to the body — walk uses of var v; if all CallClosure/TailCallClosure uses resolve via fn_constants and there are no other uses (Return, store-into-tuple, etc.), the closure value never escapes and the any-key is safe to skip.

## Tests (TDD)

- `callclosure_with_fn_constant_registers_narrow_spec`: in `apply2(double, 21)`, the queried-set for double contains `[int_lit(21)]`.
- `makeclosure_anykey_skipped_when_closure_doesnt_escape`: same fixture — `(double, [any])` NOT in queried set, so .29.12.6's drop logic removes double's any-key.
- `makeclosure_anykey_kept_when_closure_escapes`: if the closure value is returned or stored into a tuple, the any-key MUST stay registered (we can't see all consumers).
- Three-path parity: regression-pin against the .29.12 fixture set.

## Out of scope

- IR rewrite (.29.10.3). At this stage codegen still goes through the closure stub, which now dispatches to F's narrow spec rather than F's any-key — that's still correct, just goes through one more indirection.
title: Typer registers narrow F-spec from known-target CallClosure
